Sorry if that gave you a scare. Don't worry, I'm harmless.
This place here is called the "Outer Dungeons."

If this message is playing, that means that you and I are
the same... I, too, am a human, lost in the Digital World.

Through Digimon, I've come to learn about the truth of this world...
though I'm sure the same is true for everyone who wound up here.

You see, I owe so much to the Digimon here... So I've decided to
put together some games to thank them.

That was my "actual" job before I ended up here but,
of course, so much more is possible in this world.

It's kinda sad that none of them have been cleared yet,
but as a developer, it's a job well done, no?

In any case... this message will only play when
another human enters this place.

I wouldn't mind it if a fellow human were to be the first to clear them.
Well, there are plenty of rewards to be had, so why not give it a try!

Congrats on beating every last series! As a developer,
I'm truly grateful that you played through it all.

I have something I want to entrust to you. See this device?
There are Digimon sleeping in it.

Could you take care of them for me? They're a friendly and
interesting bunch.

Not long ago, a gate back to my home world opened up. I'm
thinking about returning to the human world.

I can't take any Digimon with me... People aren't ready to
accept them yet.

But, someday, I hope humans and Digimon can live together.
And, as the first step toward that...

...my plan is to make toys based on Digimon. I want to
return to the human world and pursue that dream.

Since I can't take them with me, I want to entrust you with
my Digimon. I'm sure they'll come in handy.
Well... thanks for everything. Good luck to you!

(Victory)

Explanation of the icon above one's head during card
battles.

Defeating more of the same type of Digimon will improve its
analysis and boost the Scan Rate required for Conversion.

Once the Scan Rate reaches 100%, you'll be able to Convert
that Digimon and add them to your ranks.

The closer the Scan Rate is to 200%, the stronger the new
Digimon will be, so be sure to Convert at a good time.

It's also useful for anomaly analysis, so be sure to actively
engage in combat to improve analysis and scanning.

It looks like a Digimon has been analyzed enough and is
ready for Conversion. Try the {fc9Convert} function now.

Just like how identical twins don't have the same personality,
it seems Digimon each have individual {fc9Personalities}, too.

That's from having both organic and data-like properties.
Their differences will become evident in and out of battle.

You can learn more about each Digimon's Personality under
the Setup option in the Digimon section of your Digivice.

Understanding each Digimon's Personality will be vital
to the success of your mission.

I can't predict what awaits you. Prepare for the worst, and
don't forget about {fc9Converting} and {fc9Digivolution}.

These functions are based on the theories given to us by
Dr. Yuki. Indeed, his inventions contributed greatly to society.

And yet few people know about him, due in large part to his personality. He rarely showed himself
in public, and he transferred all rights to his work to the government and large corporations.

He poured his own private funds into the establishment of
ADAMAS to protect humanity from the threat of anomalies.

With your surname of "Yuki," no one is more suited to carry
on his wishes, so master {fc9Converting} and {fc9Digivolution} well!

If you proceed, any current side mission progress will be
halted.

Be sure to first complete any ongoing side missions.
Note: {fc9Go to Digivice > Missions} for mission details.
